Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Please merge 10d in the patch from LU-5581 and 10c in this one. Approach looks good to me otherwise.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(7 comments)

Patch Set 1:

(5 comments)

Patch Set 1:

Shadow, I'd be happy to see these fixes land, but we cannot land patches that are introducing known test failures, since this would break git bisect and any other patches that may be rebased on the patch introducing the failure. 

Now that we know the tests are indeed verifying the failure case, and the later patches are fixing these problems, could you please split up the patches differently, so that the test and the fix are landed in the same patch. The patches need to pass testing in order to land.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(4 comments)

- There are still several questions from the previous version that got unanswered
- As per the checkpatch message, the patch needs to be rebased
- Someone needs to look at the maloo failure.

Hence -1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(26 comments)

It looks like the sanity.sh test failure was caused by LU-5077/LU-5079 (MDS hung due to crypto_alloc_tfm() failure), which has since been fixed on master.  Yet another reason to rebase the patch.

A number of places to fix mostly superficial issues (comments, whitespace and such).

One important question is whether this has been tested with locally-mounted clients?  I'm a bit concerned if both the forward and reverse connections are using the same client UUID.  This is actually good for normal (remote) clients, since the same UUID will be used on the server and clients and it would make finding this connection in the logs easier, so long as it doesn't cause other problems for local testing.

parents.subject: LU-7042 lnet: Handle OFED 3.18 packaging definitions

commit.subject: LU-5569 ptlrpc: change reverse import life cycle
commit.message:
LU-5569 ptlrpc: change reverse import life cycle

Make reverse import on server have same life cycle for a client
import, otherwise a reverse import disconnecting on each client
reconnect open several races in request sending (AST mostly) code.

First problem is send_rpc vs class_destroy_import() race. If send RPC
(or resending) issued after class_destroy_import() function it will
fail due to generation check.

The second problem is resending via a different router (to a different nid).
The target_handle_connect() function doesn't update the connection
information for older reverse import, so if the connection information
or security flavor has changed we won't be able to deliver an RPC
from server to the client.

The third problem is that connection flags aren't updated atomically
for an import. The target_handle_connect() function connects the new
import before the message header flags are set, so if we send an RPC
in that time it may have the wrong flags.

The final fourth problem is none wakeup an older RPC if client
reconnected to ability to resend after network flap. This was
not a problem without Vitaly's "resend AST callbacks" patch
(commit 30be03b4dd59389) as it was not possible to resend RPCs.
Now, however, this problem results in failing to resend ASTs at
all, or adding long timeout to AST RPCs.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-2038
Signed-off-by: Alexey Lyashkov <alexey_lyashkov@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: I5dd65a0a507827d6a43683dedbbc0cee263ee0d0
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/11750
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
